post_context.title: 9 Surprising Things Top Product Managers Do Differently
post_context.caption_hook: What separates top PMs isn't tech wizardry — it's how they protect clarity, users, and trust.
themes: Great PMs write the decision, not just the deck — one-page clarity beats slide theater.
They protect maker time like budget and say no without ghosting.
They talk to users weekly, instrument before celebrating, and treat onboarding as a product.
They escalate early with data and build trust in writing.
post_context.cta_question: Which of these do you already do? What would you add?
